paper_title: Radiolarian Analysis and Age Model at Site U1417

methods.description: Process for preparing and analyzing radiolarian samples from Site U1417 to determine biostratigraphic information.

steps.description: Samples were freeze-dried and then treated with diluted hydrogen peroxide (15%) and hydrochloric acid (15%) to remove organic and calcareous matter.
steps.searchable_summary: treat samples with hydrogen peroxide and hydrochloric acid to remove organic and calcareous matter

steps.description: Undissolved residues sieved over a 45 µm screen were then mounted on 22 × 40 mm cover glasses for microscopic analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: sieve residues and mount on cover glasses for analysis

steps.description: Calculated the relative abundance of biostratigraphically important radiolarian species to clarify the radiolarian biostratigraphy at this site for the Pleistocene.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate relative abundance of radiolarian species

methods.description: Examination of bioevents and construction of an age model based on radiolarian species and paleomagnetic reversal events.

steps.description: Carefully examined several well-known bioevents identified by previous studies in the North Pacific.
Calibrated radiolarian biostratigraphy at Site U1417 to paleomagnetic reversal events.
Constrained sedimentation rates for the depth interval between 111 and 186 m CCSF-B relying on paleomagnetic reversal events.
steps.searchable_summary: constrain sedimentation rates using paleomagnetic events

steps.description: Based on bioevents, determined sedimentation rates for the depth interval between 0 and 111 m CCSF-B.
